source.videoTitle: 16th July 2026 Current Affairs🔥 | Daily MCQs | Today’s Most Important MCQs #currentaffairs2026

summary:
The study material of 16th July 2026 covers several significant domestic and international developments. Domestically, the Indian Grey Hornbill has returned to Gujarat's Gir National Park after 60 years of local extinction under a species recovery programme. Additionally, the Ministry of Electronics and Information Technology (MeitY) has released the second edition of the Digital Threat Report to strengthen cyber security in the Banking, Financial Services, Insurance (BFSI) and payment ecosystem. Karnataka has announced the establishment of India's first government-driven Artificial Intelligence University, and the official mascot 'Peacock' was unveiled for the 2026 Commonwealth Table Tennis Championship in Delhi. Internationally, Ukraine and nine other European nations launched the Integrated Antiballistic Missile Coalition, while India is gearing up to host the fifth meeting of BIMSTEC National Security Chiefs in New Delhi.

This study material covers Geographical Indication (GI) tags in India for the year 2026, highlighting their legal framework, significance, and newly registered products. Regulated under the Geographical Indications of Goods Act, 1999, which came into official force in 2003, GI tags protect unique products linked to specific origins for a duration of 10 years. Currently, Uttar Pradesh leads the country with the highest number of GI products, followed closely by Tamil Nadu. Recent registrations span diverse categories from agricultural crops like Spiti Sea Buckthorn and Karbi Anglong Ginger to indigenous crafts such as Majuli Masks, Dokra Metal Craft, and Jodhpur Mojari.

Climate activist Sonam Wangchuk has been on a prolonged hunger strike, demanding constitutional safeguards and statehood for the Union Territory of Ladakh. As his health deteriorates, the Delhi High Court has intervened, emphasizing that human life is precious and directing the government to regularly monitor his health. This judicial intervention elevates the protest from a local movement to a matter of national and constitutional significance. The government now faces critical choices: continue its wait-and-watch approach, limit its response to medical monitoring, or actively initiate a political dialogue to address the core demands.
keyFacts: Indian Grey Hornbill has successfully returned to Gir National Park, Gujarat, after more than 60 years of local extinction.
MeitY released the second edition of the Digital Threat Report 2025-26 targeting BFSI cyber security.
Tamil poet and lyricist R. Vairamuthu has been conferred the 60th Jnanpith Award (for 2025), becoming the third Tamil writer to receive it.
Karnataka is setting up India's first government-driven Artificial Intelligence University.
Ukraine, along with nine other European countries, launched the Integrated Antiballistic Missile Coalition.
The Uttar Pradesh Pharmacy Council became the first State Pharmacy Council to collaborate with the Indian Pharmacopoeia Commission (IPC) to promote the National Formulary of India.
The India-UK Comprehensive Economic and Trade Agreement came into force on 15th July 2026.
Darjeeling Tea of West Bengal was the first product in India to receive a GI tag.
GI tags in India are administered by the Geographical Indication Registry under the Department for Promotion of Industry and Internal Trade (DPIIT), Ministry of Commerce and Industry.
The Geographical Indications of Goods (Registration and Protection) Act was enacted in 1999 and came into official force on September 15, 2003.
A registered Geographical Indication tag is valid for a period of 10 years, after which it can be renewed.
Uttar Pradesh currently holds the highest number of GI-tagged products in India.
The Indian Government aims to achieve a target of 10,000 GI-tagged products by the year 2030.
Sonam Wangchuk has been on a prolonged hunger strike demanding constitutional safeguards for Ladakh under the Sixth Schedule.
The Delhi High Court intervened in the matter, declaring that life is most precious and directing the government to regularly monitor Wangchuk's health.
The issue has transitioned from a localized protest to one involving judicial oversight and national media attention.
The protest highlights the ongoing political and social tensions in Ladakh following its separation from Jammu and Kashmir in 2019.
Delay in government dialogue poses a political risk of transforming a regional movement into a larger national issue.

mcqs.question: The Indian Grey Hornbill has recently returned after more than 60 years of local extinction to which of the following protected areas?
mcqs.explanation: The Indian Grey Hornbill has successfully returned to Gir National Park in Gujarat after being locally extinct for over 60 years, following a species recovery programme by the Gujarat Forest Department.

mcqs.question: Which specific sector is the primary focus of the recently released 'Digital Threat Report 2025 & 26'?

options.a: Agricultural Supply Chain and Food Storage Systems
options.b: Banking, Financial Services, Insurance (BFSI) and Payment Ecosystem

mcqs.explanation: The second edition of the Digital Threat Report 2025 & 26 primarily focuses on strengthening cyber security in the Banking, Financial Services, Insurance (BFSI) and payment ecosystem by identifying AI-driven attacks, ransomware, and payment fraud.

mcqs.question: Who has been conferred the 60th Jnanpith Award 2025, becoming the third Tamil writer to receive this prestigious literary honor?
mcqs.explanation: Tamil poet and lyricist R. Vairamuthu was conferred the 60th Jnanpith Award 2025 for his outstanding contribution to Tamil literature, making him the third Tamil writer and the first Tamil poet to receive this honour.

mcqs.question: Ukraine, along with how many other European countries, launched the newly announced Integrated Antiballistic Missile Coalition in 2026?
mcqs.explanation: Ukraine, in collaboration with nine other European countries, launched the Integrated Antiballistic Missile Coalition to develop a shared, purely defensive antiballistic missile shield to protect Europe.

mcqs.question: Which Indian state has announced the establishment of India's first government-driven Artificial Intelligence (AI) University?
mcqs.explanation: Karnataka Deputy CM D.K. Shivakumar announced the establishment of India's first government-driven Artificial Intelligence University during the Google I/O Connect India 2026 event in Bengaluru.

mcqs.question: The Indian Pharmacopoeia Commission (IPC) signed an MoU with which State Pharmacy Council to promote the National Formulary of India, making it the first state council to do so?
mcqs.explanation: The Indian Pharmacopoeia Commission (IPC) signed an MoU with the Uttar Pradesh Pharmacy Council to promote the National Formulary of India and ensure rational use of medicines, making UP the first state pharmacy council to partner for this initiative.

mcqs.question: On which date did the India-UK Comprehensive Economic and Trade Agreement officially come into action/enforcement?
mcqs.explanation: The India-UK Comprehensive Economic and Trade Agreement came into force on 15th July 2026, aimed at boosting trade, investment, and economic cooperation.

mcqs.question: Which city is set to host the fifth meeting of the BIMSTEC National Security Chiefs?
mcqs.explanation: India is set to host the fifth meeting of the BIMSTEC National Security Chiefs in New Delhi.

mcqs.question: What is the theme for the World Youth Skills Day celebrated on 15th July 2026?

options.c: Learning for People, Planet, and Prosperity

mcqs.explanation: World Youth Skills Day is observed every year on 15th July. The theme for the year 2026 is 'Skills for a Shared Future'.

mcqs.question: The 'Nidar 2.0' scheme, recently launched by the Ministry of Electronics and Information Technology (MeitY), aims to build indigenous drones using which processor?
mcqs.explanation: Under the Nidar 2.0 initiative launched by MeitY, indigenous drones will be developed using the indigenously designed Vega processor.

mcqs.question: Which space technology firm became the first company to receive investment from India's newly established Sovereign Space Tech Fund (Space Venture Capital)?
mcqs.explanation: Dhruva Space has become the first private space company to receive investment from India's Sovereign Space Tech Fund, known as the Space Venture Capital fund.

mcqs.question: What is the official mascot of the 2026 Commonwealth Table Tennis Championship to be held in New Delhi?
mcqs.explanation: The official mascot of the 2026 Commonwealth Table Tennis Championship is the Peacock (Mayur), inspired by India's national bird. The event is scheduled to be held in Delhi.

mcqs.question: Which of the following bodies is directly responsible for registering and regulating Geographical Indications (GI) in India?

options.b: Department for Promotion of Industry and Internal Trade (DPIIT)

mcqs.explanation: The Geographical Indication Registry works under the Department for Promotion of Industry and Internal Trade (DPIIT), which is part of the Ministry of Commerce and Industry.

mcqs.question: In which year did the Geographical Indications of Goods (Registration and Protection) Act, 1999 officially come into force in India?
mcqs.explanation: Although the Act was enacted by Parliament in 1999, it officially came into force on September 15, 2003.

mcqs.question: Once officially registered, for how many years is a Geographical Indication (GI) tag valid in India before requiring renewal?
mcqs.explanation: A registered Geographical Indication (GI) tag is valid for a period of 10 years and must be renewed periodically after that.

mcqs.question: As of mid-2026, which Indian state holds the rank-one position with the highest number of registered GI products?
mcqs.explanation: Uttar Pradesh holds the top position in India with the highest number of registered GI products, followed by Tamil Nadu.

mcqs.question: The handcrafted leather footwear known as 'Jodhpur Mojari' has recently been granted a GI tag. Which state does it belong to?
mcqs.explanation: Jodhpur Mojari is a handcrafted leather footwear traditional to the Marwar region of Rajasthan, which secured the GI tag.

mcqs.question: The 'Balagarh Traditional Boat', which recently received a GI tag, is a handcrafted wooden boat originating from which state?
mcqs.explanation: The Balagarh Traditional Boat is a handcrafted wooden boat originating from the Hooghly district of West Bengal.

mcqs.question:
Consider the following traditional crafts of Jharkhand that received GI tags in 2026:
1. Dokra Craft
2. Jadopatiya Painting
3. Munda Jewellery
Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
mcqs.explanation: All three crafts (Dokra metal craft, Jadopatiya painting, and Munda jewellery) are part of the traditional items of Jharkhand that received GI tags in 2026.

mcqs.question: The traditional musical instrument 'Sarinda', which secured a GI tag, belongs to which of the following North-Eastern states?
mcqs.explanation: Sarinda is a traditional, multi-stringed musical instrument originating from Tripura that has been granted a GI tag.

mcqs.question: The historical 'Bhavan Booti Saree' is a handloom textile that was granted a GI tag. Which state does it belong to?
mcqs.explanation: Bhavan Booti Saree is a traditional handloom textile made of cotton or wool from the Nalanda region of Bihar.

mcqs.question: The traditional wall and floor art originating from the Bhojpuri region, known as 'Pedia Painting', belongs to which state?
mcqs.explanation: Pedia Painting is an ancient traditional wall and floor art from the Bhojpuri region of Bihar that recently earned a GI tag.

mcqs.question: With reference to the cultural items of Assam, 'Bihu Pepa' is a newly GI-tagged:
mcqs.explanation: Bihu Pepa is a traditional musical instrument from Assam that has been granted GI tag protection.

mcqs.question: 'Jasdan Pathri', which recently received a GI tag, is a traditional handcrafted wooden chest (box) originating from which state?
mcqs.explanation: Jasdan Pathri is a traditional handcrafted wooden chest (box) from the Rajkot district of Gujarat.

mcqs.question: Which agricultural crop, famous for its intense health benefits and known as 'Wonder Berry', received a GI tag from the cold deserts of Himachal Pradesh?
mcqs.explanation: Spiti Sea Buckthorn is a nutrient-rich wonder berry growing in the cold deserts of Spiti, Himachal Pradesh, which has received a GI tag.

mcqs.question: The aromatic and nutrient-rich 'Thoyamali Rice', which recently secured a GI tag, is primarily cultivated in which state?
mcqs.explanation: Thoyamali Rice is a famous traditional, aromatic, and nutrient-rich rice variety cultivated in Tamil Nadu.

mcqs.question: 'Amba Samudram Chopu Samaan', which received a GI tag, is famous for which category of traditional crafts?
mcqs.explanation: Amba Samudram Chopu Samaan from Tamil Nadu comprises traditional, handcrafted wooden miniature kitchen utensils and toys.

mcqs.question: The 'Majuli Mask' and 'Majuli Manuscript Paintings', which received GI tags, belong to which Indian state?
mcqs.explanation: Majuli masks (made of bamboo and clay) and Majuli manuscript paintings depicting Vaishnavite traditions are from the river island of Majuli in Assam.

mcqs.question: 'Sitahi Kutki' and 'Nagdaman Kutki' are newly GI-tagged nutrient-rich millets cultivated by tribal communities in which state?
mcqs.explanation: Sitahi Kutki and Nagdaman Kutki are small, nutritious indigenous millets adapted to rainfed farming in Madhya Pradesh.

mcqs.question: Which schedule of the Indian Constitution contains provisions regarding the administration of tribal areas in the states of Assam, Meghalaya, Tripura, and Mizoram, which is also a key demand for Ladakh's administration?
mcqs.explanation: The Sixth Schedule of the Indian Constitution provides for the administration of tribal areas in Assam, Meghalaya, Tripura, and Mizoram to safeguard the rights of these tribal populations. Civil society groups in Ladakh, led by figures like Sonam Wangchuk, are demanding inclusion under this schedule.

mcqs.question: The Delhi High Court's statement that 'life is most precious' during Sonam Wangchuk's fast closely aligns with which Fundamental Right guaranteed under the Constitution of India?

options.c: Article 21 (Right to Life and Personal Liberty)

mcqs.explanation: Article 21 of the Indian Constitution guarantees the Protection of Life and Personal Liberty, stating that no person shall be deprived of his life or personal liberty except according to procedure established by law. The High Court's emphasis on preserving life directly resonates with this article.

mcqs.question: In the context of the Indian federal structure, what is the primary constitutional difference between a State and a Union Territory?

options.a: States are administered directly by the President, while Union Territories have independent governors.
options.b: States have their own elected governments with legislative powers over state subjects, whereas Union Territories are directly governed by the Central Government (unless specified otherwise by law).
options.c: Union Territories have their own high courts, whereas states share high courts.
options.d: Only states can have representation in the Rajya Sabha.

mcqs.explanation: States are constituent units of the Indian federal system and enjoy legislative and executive powers distributed by the Constitution. Union Territories are administrative units controlled directly by the Central Government through an Administrator or Lieutenant Governor, though some like Delhi and Puducherry have legislatures.

mcqs.question: Which article of the Indian Constitution empowers the Parliament to form a new State or alter the boundaries/names of existing States, which was utilized during the reorganization of Jammu and Kashmir in 2019?
mcqs.explanation: Article 3 of the Indian Constitution authorizes Parliament to form new states, increase or decrease the area of any state, alter the boundaries, or change the name of any state through a simple majority.

mcqs.question: Under the Sixth Schedule of the Indian Constitution, which institutions are created to administer tribal areas with legislative, judicial, and administrative autonomy?
mcqs.explanation: The Sixth Schedule provides for the creation of Autonomous District Councils (ADCs) and Regional Councils, which have powers to make laws on subjects like land, forests, water, agriculture, and local customs within their jurisdictions.

mcqs.question: In judicial review, when a High Court issues directions to the government regarding the health of a protesting citizen, it is primarily exercising which of its jurisdictions?
mcqs.explanation: Under Article 226 of the Constitution, High Courts have wide powers of writ jurisdiction to issue directions, orders, or writs to any person or authority, including the government, for the enforcement of fundamental rights and other legal rights.

mcqs.question: Which of the following bodies is responsible for officially advising the President of India regarding the administration and constitutional status of Scheduled Areas?
mcqs.explanation: The National Commission for Scheduled Tribes (NCST), a constitutional body under Article 338A, is tasked with overseeing the implementation of safeguards for Scheduled Tribes and advising on their developmental and constitutional rights.

mcqs.question: In the history of Indian democracy, fasts and hunger strikes as a method of political protest were popularized primarily during the freedom struggle by which leader?
mcqs.explanation: Mahatma Gandhi popularized Satyagraha and hunger strikes (fasting) as powerful ethical and political tools of non-violent resistance against British rule.

mcqs.question: Which constitutional amendment act facilitated the creation of the National Commission for Scheduled Tribes (NCST) as a separate constitutional body?
mcqs.explanation: The 89th Constitutional Amendment Act of 2003 bifurcated the erstwhile combined National Commission for Scheduled Castes and Scheduled Tribes into two separate bodies: the NCSC (Article 338) and the NCST (Article 338A).

mcqs.question: Which of the following acts protects the ecological and cultural identity of local communities while managing land and resource rights in tribal-dominated areas of mainland India?
mcqs.explanation: The Provisions of the Panchayats (Extension to the Scheduled Areas) Act, 1996 (PESA) extends Panchayat rule to Fifth Schedule areas, granting tribal communities significant control over local resources and self-governance.
